Skip to content
Discord Next

DiscordJS NextDiscord Bots at Next.

File-based routing, native developer tools, Hot Module Replacement, and built-in safety nets — all in one install.

Get Started →
API Reference

File-Based Routing

Auto-loads commands, events, components, and tasks natively. Supports dynamic routes like ban_user_[id].js.

Learn more

Native Developer Tools

A built-in developer suite inside Discord. Evaluate JS live, restart the bot, or check heap usage instantly.

View commands

Hot Module Replacement

Live-reload your commands, events, and logic on save without ever dropping your Discord Gateway connection.

Database Injection

Connect Prisma, Mongoose, or Supabase with full TypeScript generics directly into the framework core.

See examples

Persistent Cooldowns

Plug in a custom cache adapter like Redis for global persistent cooldown tracking across restarts.

Built-in Pagination

Ship beautiful interactive paginated embeds out-of-the-box using the native PaginationBuilder utility.

API docs

Get started in seconds:
$ npx djs-next init

File-Based Magic

Drop a file. Get a command. No massive switch statements required.

EXPLORER
src
commands
JS ping.js
events
JS ready.js
components
JS ban_user.js
Live Preview
maps to /ping
/ping
DiscordJS Next BOT

Pong! Latency is 14ms.

Write Less. Do More.

See the difference between Vanilla Discord.js and djs-next.

const { GatewayIntentBits, DJSNextClient } = require('djs-next');

const client = new DJSNextClient({
  intents: [GatewayIntentBits.Guilds],
  developers: ['YOUR_ID'],
  prefixes: ['!'],
});

client.enableHMR();
client.enableDevTools('dnxt');
client.start(process.env.DISCORD_TOKEN);

// That's it. Commands, events, components,
// cooldowns, permissions, error handling —
// all handled automatically by dropping
// files into src/commands/, src/events/,
// and src/components/.
